title: Tabernaemontana odontadeniiflora
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana odontadeniiflora is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Tabernaemontana is a part of this family and is native to tropical regions of Asia. The species odontadeniiflora is further classified within the genus Tabernaemontana. Further classification of the species within the family and genus is needed for a more detailed understanding of its taxonomic position.

intro: Tabernaemontana odontadeniiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the poison tree due to its toxic properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana odontadeniiflora include alkaloids such as tabernaemontanine and odontadenine, which have been found to possess antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Tabernaemontana odontadeniiflora is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
